title: paramignya trimera
intro: Paramignya trimera, also known as the "Wild Orange of Borneo", is a plant species native to the rainforests of Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Paramignya trimera include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent wound infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits are often used to make teas, infusions, and traditional remedies, such as tinctures and decoctions, to harness its medicinal properties.
